name: Factory Droid

form_factor.comment: Droid CLI plus Factory App, IDE integrations, and Droid Computers. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://docs.factory.ai/reference/cli-reference; Extension: https://docs.factory.ai/integrations/ide-integrations; Web: https://app.factory.ai/.

released_in.comment: Public docs cover current Droid CLI and Factory platform surfaces.

latest_major_update.comment: June 2026 Factory model/docs refresh: available-models guidance updated with new managed and Droid Core coding models, reasoning effort options, multipliers, and current CLI defaults.

rules.comment: AGENTS.md appears in Factory configuration docs. Location: `AGENTS.md` and `.factory/` project configuration.

skills.comment: Skills can be managed and invoked from the CLI. Location: Factory Skills configuration in project/user Factory config; exact folder varies by scope.

hooks.comment: Lifecycle hooks are first-class slash-command managed features.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports stdio and HTTP MCP servers, registry browsing, env vars, and headers. Location: user `~/.factory/mcp.json` and project MCP configuration.

custom_commands.comment: Custom slash commands are directly managed in the CLI. Location: Factory custom slash-command configuration in project/user Factory config.

goal_command.comment: Factory documents custom slash commands via `/commands`; no built-in `/goal` for persistent objectives.

subagents.comment: Custom Droids are documented as subagents.

monitor.comment: Droid has lifecycle hooks, custom Droids, and headless exec flows, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated background monitor/watch primitive.

model_selection.comment: Model IDs can be selected with -m/--model and /model.

approval_mode.comment: Tiered autonomy levels control action permissions.

sandbox_mode.comment: Docs include Sandbox Beta and unsafe mode guidance for disposable containers.

resume.comment: Resume by session ID or last modified session.

continue.comment: Exec can continue an existing session; interactive defaults to the last modified session.

non_interactive.comment: droid exec is the headless automation mode.

output_format.comment: Search supports JSON output; API and readiness endpoints expose structured data.

telemetry.comment: Analytics/compliance APIs exist, but no user telemetry opt-out setting found.

hosted_agent.comment: Factory App and Droid Computers exist; CLI can register machines and run daemon/server flows.

custom_model_provider.comment: Bring Your Own Key/custom model path is documented.

pricing.value: Pro $20/mo; Plus $100/mo; Max $200/mo; Teams custom; Extra Usage prepaid $10 minimum; Droid Core free
pricing.comment: Factory docs list Pro, Plus, Max, Teams, Droid Core, and prepaid Extra Usage; Droid CLI/App/SDK are included in the paid Factory plans.

no_account_required.comment: Droid BYOK supports local models, but normal Droid CLI setup still documents Factory authentication, so account-free use is not confirmed end-to-end.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Droid BYOK supports external provider keys, open-source providers, and local models.

notes: One of the most feature-dense CLI surfaces in this update: autonomy levels, worktrees, custom droids, plugins, MCP, statusline, skills, hooks, and headless exec.

transcripts.comment: Location: local searchable Droid session data is managed by Factory CLI; exact transcript database path is not documented in the CLI reference.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Research needed. Check official documentation for keybinding customization options.

theme_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for theme/appearance customization options.

auto_save.comment: Research needed. Check for auto-save or session persistence features.

debugging_output.comment: Research needed. Check for debug mode or verbose logging options.

context_window.comment: Research needed. Check for configurable context length or token limits.

code_formatting.comment: Research needed. Check for formatter/linter integration or auto-format options.

git_integration.comment: Research needed. Check for git command support or version control integration.

performance_mode.comment: Research needed. Check for caching, optimization, or resource management settings.

ui_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for UI layout, sidebar, or panel customization.

git_worktrees.comment: Runs as a local process, so it works inside a manually created git worktree for parallel isolation, but ships no built-in worktree manager.

chat_forking.comment: No public documentation found for forking a conversation into a separate branch/session.

done_notifications.comment: No public documentation found for built-in completion/attention notifications.

byok_prompt_caching.comment: Supports BYOK; provider-side caching can apply when routing to Anthropic/OpenAI with your key, but caching behavior is not separately documented.
